name: Orange Cream Brownies
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Butter (1 stick) cut in pieces
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 325°F. Line a 9-inch square baking pan with foil, allowing it to overhang slightly. Butter the foil. Combine flour, baking powder, and salt on a square of wax paper.
In a small saucepan, melt chocolate pieces, butter, sugar, and orange juice over very low heat, stirring until smooth. Remove from heat. Stir in orange peel and vanilla extract. Beat eggs one at a time into the mixture, then add the reserved flour mixture, beating until smooth. Stir in nuts and spread the batter evenly in the prepared pan.
Bake for 23-25 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean. Cool completely on a wire rack. Spread with orange cream. Using a knife, mark chocolate icing into 36 (1 1/2") squares. Refrigerate until the icing is firm (about 25 minutes or place in freezer for 5 minutes).
Remove from foil and cut into squares. Refrigerate up to 4 days or wrap tightly and freeze up to 1 month.
Orange Cream:
In an electric mixer, beat butter until creamy. Gradually add sugar, beating until fluffy. Beat in orange peel, milk, and vanilla extract. Makes 1 1/2 cups.
Chocolate Icing:
In a small saucepan, combine chocolate pieces, butter, and vegetable shortening. Stir over low heat until melted. Makes 1 cup.
